title: drymocallis glandulosa subsp. globosa
intro: Drymocallis glandulosa subsp. globosa, also known as globe-leaved twinflower, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to western North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. It is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Drymocallis glandulosa subsp. globosa can be prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
